message:
feat: streaming JSONL parser, minified-bundle scanner fix, and conformance test hardening

Session JSONL loading (src/session.rs):
- Replace read_to_string + lines() with BufReader + lines() for both
  open_jsonl_with_diagnostics and load_session_meta_jsonl, so large
  session files are streamed line-by-line instead of buffered entirely
  in memory. This reduces peak RSS for multi-MB session files.
- open_jsonl_with_diagnostics now runs the blocking I/O on a dedicated
  thread via oneshot channel + AgentCx, keeping the async runtime
  unblocked.

Extension compatibility scanner (src/extensions.rs):
- Add LONG_LINE_COMMENT_BYPASS_LEN (4096 chars): when a source line
  exceeds this threshold and comment stripping shortened it, the scanner
  falls back to raw text instead. This prevents minified bundles with
  regex literals from confusing the line-oriented comment stripper and
  dropping child_process require() calls that occur after a false
  comment-open sequence.
- New test: compatibility_scanner_keeps_late_requires_in_minified_lines
  verifies the doom.js bundle correctly infers exec capability.

Conformance test harness (tests/conformance_report.rs):
- Extract generate_conformance_report_impl() so the report generator can
  be called both from the #[test] entry point and from helper functions.
- Add CONFORMANCE_REPORT_IO_LOCK (LazyLock<Mutex>) to serialize tests
  that read/write shared report files, preventing flaky parallel-test
  failures.
- read_or_regenerate_conformance_events() auto-generates the report if
  the events file is missing, removing early-return skips from
  evidence_links_valid and events_jsonl_has_capabilities tests.

Whitespace edit test (tests/reproduce_edit_whitespace.rs):
- Update expected behavior: "baz " is now recognized as an exact
  substring match at the beginning of "baz  ", so the result is "qux "
  (one trailing space) rather than "qux  " (two trailing spaces from
  the old fuzzy-match path).

Other:
- tests/suite_classification.toml: add reproduce_bom_bug and
  reproduce_edit_whitespace to the classified test list.
- Regenerate extension-conformance-test-plan.json, extension-inclusion-list.json,
  VALIDATED_MANIFEST.json, conformance reports, perf budgets, and bench
  schema docs with current timestamps.
- New BENCH_SCHEMA.md documents all registered benchmark JSONL schemas
  and their required fields.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-346","title":"Workstream: Session Index Integration","description":"# Workstream: Session Index Integration

## Goal
Wire the existing SQLite session index (src/session_index.rs) into the CLI flow
for fast session discovery and resumption.

## Background
The session index module is fully implemented (360+ lines) but not used:
- SQLite-based index with WAL mode
- Fast lookups by path or CWD
- Session metadata extraction (message count, name, file size, mtime)
- Atomic operations with file locking

Currently, session operations scan the filesystem directly, which is slow
for users with many sessions.

## Current State

### What Exists (src/session_index.rs)
```rust
pub struct SessionIndex { db: SqliteConnection }

impl SessionIndex {
    pub fn open(path: &Path) -> Result<Self>
    pub fn index_session(&self, session_path: &Path) -> Result<()>
    pub fn list_sessions(&self, cwd: Option<&str>) -> Result<Vec<SessionMeta>>
    pub fn find_recent(&self, cwd: &str) -> Result<Option<SessionMeta>>
    pub fn reindex_all(&self) -> Result<()>
}

pub struct SessionMeta {
    pub path: PathBuf,
    pub cwd: String,
    pub name: Option<String>,
    pub message_count: u32,
    pub created_at: DateTime,
    pub modified_at: DateTime,
    pub file_size: u64,
}
```

### What's Missing
1. No calls to `index_session()` after session.persist()
2. No calls to `list_sessions()` in session picker
3. No calls to `find_recent()` for --continue flag
4. No index maintenance hooks

## Implementation Plan

### Phase 1: Wire Index on Session Save
- After session.persist(), call session_index.index_session()
- Index path stored at ~/.pi/agent/sessions/index.db

### Phase 2: Use Index for Session Listing
- --resume flag queries index instead of filesystem walk
- Session picker uses indexed metadata
- Much faster for users with 100+ sessions

### Phase 3: Use Index for --continue
- --continue queries index for most recent by CWD
- Falls back to filesystem if index empty/stale

### Phase 4: Index Maintenance
- Background reindex on startup (if stale)
- Handle deleted sessions (prune index)
- Handle moved sessions (update paths)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Sessions indexed on save
- [ ] Session picker uses index
- [ ] --continue uses index
- [ ] Startup remains fast (<100ms)
- [ ] Handles index corruption gracefully

## Files Affected
- src/session_index.rs (already implemented)
- src/session.rs (add indexing calls)
- src/session_picker.rs (use index queries)
- src/main.rs (wire up index)","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss

 {"id":"bd-jt3k","title":"Task: Wire Hostcall Dispatcher into Agent Loop","description":"# Task: Wire Hostcall Dispatcher into Agent Loop

## Objective

Integrate the ExtensionDispatcher into src/agent.rs so that hostcalls are processed during the agent's execution cycle. THIS IS THE CRITICAL INTEGRATION POINT.

## Background

The agent loop (src/agent.rs) orchestrates:
1. Building context (system prompt, messages, tools)
2. Calling provider.stream() to get model response
3. Processing stream events (text, thinking, tool calls)
4. Executing tools and appending results
5. Iterating until done

The extension runtime runs alongside this but is currently disconnected. We need to:
1. Initialize the extension runtime on agent start
2. Process pending hostcalls after each JS tick
3. Handle async hostcall execution without blocking streaming
4. Clean up on agent shutdown

## Current Agent Structure (src/agent.rs)

```rust
impl Agent {
    pub async fn run(
        &self,
        initial_message: Option<&str>,
        on_event: impl Fn(AgentEvent),
    ) -> Result<Vec<Message>> {
        // ... message handling loop
        loop {
            // Build context
            // Call provider.stream()
            // Process events
            // Execute tools
            // [MISSING: Process extension hostcalls]
        }
    }
}
```

## Implementation Plan

### 1. Add ExtensionDispatcher to Agent

```rust
pub struct Agent {
    provider: Arc<dyn Provider>,
    tools: Arc<ToolRegistry>,
    config: AgentConfig,
    // NEW: Extension runtime and dispatcher
    extension_runtime: Option<Arc<PiJsRuntime>>,
    extension_dispatcher: Option<Arc<ExtensionDispatcher>>,
}
```

### 2. Initialize Extensions on Agent Creation

```rust
impl Agent {
    pub async fn new_with_extensions(
        provider: Arc<dyn Provider>,
        tools: Arc<ToolRegistry>,
        config: AgentConfig,
        extension_paths: Vec<PathBuf>,
        session: Arc<dyn ExtensionSession>,
        ui_handler: Arc<dyn ExtensionUiHandler>,
    ) -> Result<Self> {
        // Load extensions
        let runtime = Arc::new(PiJsRuntime::new().await?);
        
        for path in &extension_paths {
            runtime.load_extension(path).await?;
        }
        
        // Create dispatcher
        let dispatcher = Arc::new(ExtensionDispatcher::new(
            runtime.clone(),
            tools.clone(),
            HttpConnector::new(),
            session,
            ui_handler,
            config.cwd.clone(),
        ));
        
        Ok(Self {
            provider,
            tools,
            config,
            extension_runtime: Some(runtime),
            extension_dispatcher: Some(dispatcher),
        })
    }
}
```

### 3. Process Hostcalls in Agent Loop

```rust
impl Agent {
    async fn process_extension_hostcalls(&self) -> Result<()> {
        if let Some(dispatcher) = &self.extension_dispatcher {
            dispatcher.process_pending().await?;
        }
        Ok(())
    }
    
    pub async fn run(...) -> Result<Vec<Message>> {
        loop {
            // ... existing loop body ...
            
            // Process any pending extension hostcalls
            self.process_extension_hostcalls().await?;
            
            // ... continue loop ...
        }
    }
}
```

### 4. Call Points for Hostcall Processing

Hostcalls should be processed at these points:
1. After extension initialization (activate() may make hostcalls)
2. After each tool execution (tools may trigger extension hooks)
3. After processing user input (input hooks may make hostcalls)
4. During idle periods (timer-based hostcalls)

## Async Execution Concerns

**Problem**: Hostcalls may be async (HTTP requests, tool execution) and shouldn't block streaming.

**Solution**: Use asupersync's structured concurrency:

```rust
async fn process_pending(&self) -> Result<()> {
    let requests = self.runtime.drain_hostcall_requests();
    
    // Spawn each hostcall as a concurrent task
    let handles: Vec<_> = requests.into_iter().map(|req| {
        let dispatcher = self.clone();
        asupersync::spawn(async move {
            let outcome = dispatcher.dispatch(req.kind).await;
            (req.call_id, outcome)
        })
    }).collect();
    
    // Wait for all and complete
    for handle in handles {
        let (call_id, outcome) = handle.await;
        self.runtime.complete_hostcall(&call_id, outcome)?;
    }
    
    Ok(())
}
```

## Error Handling

- Hostcall errors should NOT crash the agent
- Errors return as HostcallOutcome::Error
- JS Promise rejects, extension handles error
- Log errors for debugging

## Testing Strategy

1. Unit test: Agent with extensions initializes correctly
2. Unit test: Hostcalls processed after tool execution
3. Integration test: Extension tool call works end-to-end
4. Integration test: Extension HTTP call works
5. Stress test: Many concurrent hostcalls

## Dependencies

- Depends on: bd-389q (ExtensionDispatcher struct)
- Depends on: All hostcall handler tasks
- Blocks: End-to-end extension functionality

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Agent can be created with extension support
- [ ] Extensions loaded and activated on startup
- [ ] Hostcalls processed at appropriate points in loop
- [ ] Async hostcalls don't block streaming
- [ ] Errors handled gracefully

 {"id":"bd-k5q5.2.10","title":"Conformance report integrity: eliminate stale smoke artifacts causing false FAILs for dynamic-resources/git-checkpoint/status-line","description":"Fix report-source inconsistency where conformance aggregation marks dynamic-resources, git-checkpoint, and status-line as FAIL by reading stale smoke artifacts under tests/ext_conformance/reports/smoke/extensions even when current extension scenario outputs are PASS under tests/ext_conformance/reports/extensions. Ensure aggregation and evidence contract consume deterministic, current-run artifacts only and cannot regress due to stale files from prior runs.","design":"Reproduce failing signal from conformance_events.jsonl and compare per-extension outputs in reports/smoke/extensions vs reports/extensions. Add guardrails so stale artifacts are ignored or purged from selection logic. This discrepancy likely inflates FAIL counts and undermines confidence gates.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-08T03:58:51.159590476Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T04:05:07.841043379Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T04:05:07.840906313Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","harness","reports","smoke"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.2.10","depends_on_id":"bd-k5q5.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T03:58:51.159590476Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":819,"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.2.10","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Fixed stale smoke artifacts causing false FAILs for dynamic-resources/git-checkpoint/status-line.

+# Benchmark JSONL Schema Reference
+
+> Auto-generated. Do not edit manually.
+
+## Registered Schemas
+
+| Schema | Description |
+|---|---|
+| `pi.ext.rust_bench.v1` | Rust QuickJS extension benchmark event (load, tool call, event hook) |
+| `pi.ext.legacy_bench.v1` | Legacy pi-mono (Node.js) extension benchmark event |
+| `pi.perf.workload.v1` | PiJS workload harness output (tool call throughput) |
+| `pi.perf.budget.v1` | Performance budget check result |
+| `pi.perf.budget_summary.v1` | Aggregate budget summary with pass/fail counts |
+| `pi.ext.conformance_report.v2` | Per-extension conformance report event |
+| `pi.ext.conformance_summary.v2` | Aggregate conformance summary with per-tier breakdowns |
+
+## Environment Fingerprint
+
+Every benchmark record SHOULD include an `env` object with:
+
+| Field | Type | Description |
+|---|---|---|
+| `os` | string | Operating system name and version |
+| `arch` | string | CPU architecture (x86_64, aarch64) |
+| `cpu_model` | string | CPU model string from /proc/cpuinfo or sysinfo |
+| `cpu_cores` | integer | Logical CPU core count |
+| `mem_total_mb` | integer | Total system memory in megabytes |
+| `build_profile` | string | Cargo build profile: debug or release |
+| `git_commit` | string | Short git commit hash of the build |
+| `features` | string[] | Active Cargo feature flags |
+| `config_hash` | string | SHA-256 of env fields for dedup |
+
+## Required Fields by Schema
+
+### `pi.ext.rust_bench.v1`
+
+| Field | Type | Description |
+|---|---|---|
+| `schema` | string | Always `"pi.ext.rust_bench.v1"` |
+| `runtime` | string | Always `"pi_agent_rust"` |
+| `scenario` | string | Benchmark scenario (e.g., `ext_load_init/load_init_cold`) |
+| `extension` | string | Extension ID being benchmarked |
+| `runs` | integer | Number of runs (load scenarios) |
+| `iterations` | integer | Number of iterations (throughput scenarios) |
+| `summary` | object | `{count, min_ms, p50_ms, p95_ms, p99_ms, max_ms}` |
